### Arms

Before his succession, George was granted the [royal arms](/wiki/Royal_arms "Royal arms") differenced by a [label](/wiki/Label_(heraldry) "Label (heraldry)") of five points [Azure](/wiki/Azure_(heraldry) "Azure (heraldry)"), the centre point bearing a [fleur-de-lis](/wiki/Fleur-de-lis "Fleur-de-lis") [Or](/wiki/Or_(heraldry) "Or (heraldry)") on 27 July 1749. Upon his father's death, and along with the dukedom of Edinburgh and the position of heir-apparent, he inherited his difference of a plain label of three points [Argent](/wiki/Argent "Argent"). In an additional difference, the crown of Charlemagne was not usually depicted on the [arms of the heir](/wiki/Coat_of_arms_of_the_Prince_of_Wales "Coat of arms of the Prince of Wales"), only on the Sovereign's.[[157]](#cite_note-163)

From his succession until 1800, George bore the royal arms: [Quarterly](/wiki/Quartering_(heraldry) "Quartering (heraldry)"), I [Gules](/wiki/Gules "Gules") three lions [passant guardant](/wiki/Passant_guardant "Passant guardant") in [pale](/wiki/Pale_(heraldry) "Pale (heraldry)") Or ([for England](/wiki/Royal_Arms_of_England "Royal Arms of England")) [impaling](/wiki/Impalement_(heraldry) "Impalement (heraldry)") Or a lion [rampant](/wiki/Rampant "Rampant") within a [tressure](/wiki/Tressure "Tressure") flory-counter-flory Gules ([for Scotland](/wiki/Royal_coat_of_arms_of_Scotland "Royal coat of arms of Scotland")); II Azure three fleurs-de-lys Or (for France); III Azure a harp Or stringed Argent ([for Ireland](/wiki/Coat_of_arms_of_Ireland "Coat of arms of Ireland")); IV [tierced per pale and per chevron](/wiki/Division_of_the_field "Division of the field") (for Hanover), I Gules two lions passant guardant Or (for Brunswick), II Or a [semy](/wiki/Semy "Semy") of hearts Gules a lion rampant Azure (for Lüneburg), III Gules a horse [courant](/wiki/Attitude_(heraldry)#Courant "Attitude (heraldry)") Argent ([for Saxony](/wiki/Saxon_Steed "Saxon Steed")), overall an escutcheon Gules charged with the [crown of Charlemagne](/wiki/Imperial_Crown_of_the_Holy_Roman_Empire "Imperial Crown of the Holy Roman Empire") Or (for the dignity of Archtreasurer of the Holy Roman Empire).[[158]](#cite_note-164)[[159]](#cite_note-165)

Following the [Acts of Union 1800](/wiki/Acts_of_Union_1800 "Acts of Union 1800"), the royal arms were amended, dropping the French quartering. They became: Quarterly, I and IV England; II Scotland; III Ireland; overall an escutcheon of Hanover surmounted by an electoral bonnet.[[160]](#cite_note-166) In 1816, after the [Electorate of Hanover](/wiki/Electorate_of_Hanover "Electorate of Hanover") became a kingdom, the electoral bonnet was changed to a crown.[[161]](#cite_note-167)

## Notes

1. **[^](#cite_ref-note_1_1-0)** King of the United Kingdom from 1 January 1801, after the [Acts of Union 1800](/wiki/Acts_of_Union_1800 "Acts of Union 1800")
2. **[^](#cite_ref-note_2_2-0)** King from 12 October 1814
3. ^ [***a***](#cite_ref-date_3-0) [***b***](#cite_ref-date_3-1) All dates in this article are in the [New Style](/wiki/New_Style "New Style") [Gregorian calendar](/wiki/Gregorian_calendar "Gregorian calendar"). George was born on 24 May in the Old Style [Julian calendar](/wiki/Julian_calendar "Julian calendar") used in Great Britain until 1752.
4. **[^](#cite_ref-23)** George was falsely said to have married [Hannah Lightfoot](/wiki/Hannah_Lightfoot "Hannah Lightfoot"), a [Quaker](/wiki/Quaker "Quaker"), on 17 April 1759, prior to his marriage to Charlotte, and to have had at least one child by her. However, Lightfoot had married Isaac Axford in 1753, and had died in or before 1759, so there could have been no legal marriage or children. The jury at the 1866 trial of [Lavinia Ryves](/wiki/Lavinia_Ryves "Lavinia Ryves"), the daughter of imposter [Olivia Serres](/wiki/Olivia_Serres "Olivia Serres") who pretended to be "Princess Olive of Cumberland", unanimously found that a supposed marriage certificate produced by Ryves was a forgery.[[19]](#cite_note-22)
5. **[^](#cite_ref-31)** For example, the letters of [Horace Walpole](/wiki/Horace_Walpole "Horace Walpole") written at the time of the accession defended George but Walpole's later memoirs were hostile.[[26]](#cite_note-30)
6. **[^](#cite_ref-48)** An American taxpayer would pay a maximum of sixpence a year, compared to an average of twenty-five shillings (50 times as much) in England.[[41]](#cite_note-46) In 1763, the total revenue from America amounted to about £1,800, while the estimated annual cost of the military in America was put at £225,000. By 1767, it had risen to £400,000.[[42]](#cite_note-47)
